briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. I will now begin the climate briefing for March 7, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Accelerating Global Warming and Its Impacts
sections.content: According to recent research, global warming has accelerated more than ever in the past decade, with the warming trend nearly doubling since 2014 [11]. Scientists warn that global warming has reached its fastest pace in 100 years, reporting a 'significant' acceleration in warming since 2015 [17, 23]. This acceleration is leading to direct threats, such as an increased risk of avalanches [16], and is even causing technical issues, such as adverse effects on electric vehicle batteries [9].

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Spreading Carbon Neutrality Practices in Korea
sections.content: In Korea, efforts to practice carbon neutrality are actively underway, centered around local governments and educational institutions. Jeonbuk Province and Gwangju City are respectively recruiting participants for the Carbon Neutrality Point System and the Vehicle Carbon Neutrality Point System on a first-come, first-served basis, encouraging voluntary participation from citizens [1, 2]. In Dongdaemun-gu, the 4th cohort of the Youth Climate Volunteer Group has launched, leading carbon neutrality practices alongside young people [3], and Gwangmyeong City is fostering '1.5℃ Climate Volunteers' with the goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2050 [4]. Furthermore, the Chungbuk Provincial Office of Education is operating all schools in the province as carbon-neutral schools, strengthening climate crisis response education [5].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic Renewable Energy Transition Efforts and Challenges
sections.content: In Korea, investments and efforts for the transition to renewable energy are also continuing. Taean County has signed a 1 trillion won renewable energy investment agreement with Vina Group, accelerating the region's energy transition [8], and Yeonggwang County is sharing the profits generated from renewable energy generation with its residents, distributing the benefits of the energy transition [6]. However, an imbalance in media coverage has emerged, with central newspapers primarily covering nuclear power while local newspapers focus on renewable energy, revealing the stark reality of climate reporting and underscoring the importance of providing balanced information [7].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Response Efforts and Knowledge Sharing
sections.content: The international community is exploring knowledge sharing and policy strengthening to address climate change. In Nairobi, Kenya, a workshop was held to validate a new climate knowledge portal aimed at improving access to climate-related information, facilitating discussions for stakeholders to evaluate it [15, 22]. The President of Finland urged a joint response to climate change, emphasizing the importance of international cooperation [20], and Kenya's Ministry of Environment and Climate Change concluded a technical consultation workshop for the first phase review of the national environmental policy [18]. Notably, in India's Punjab region, women are leading the fight against air pollution and climate change, demonstrating the power of grassroots movements [21].

sections.title: ⚠️ The Need for Climate Change Adaptation and Awareness Raising
sections.content: The necessity for adaptation to accelerating climate change and raising awareness is also being highlighted. Realistic adaptation measures are required, such as the proposal to purchase land in safe areas as part of insurance policies to prepare for climate change [10]. Concerns are also being raised about the impact of climate change on U.S. coastal cities [14], and reflection is needed on the reality that the perception of 'we are all in this together' does not always translate into actual climate action [12]. Given the complexity and widespread impacts of climate change, continuous education and active encouragement of participation are crucial at this juncture.

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, global warming is accelerating, and alongside diverse domestic and international response efforts, more robust implementation and adaptation strategies are required. This concludes today's climate briefing.
